$comment: VERIFY-1 frozen-design artifact. Strict superset of write-gdd/gdd.schema.json: carries all gdd fields (config now REQUIRED) and adds layout/coupling/feasibility/referenceSolution/acceptanceCriteria/declaredRanges/verdict. declaredRanges uses a FLAT MAP: { "<parameterPath>": [min, max] } where parameterPath is a dotted string (e.g. "config.walkSpeed" or "layout.rewards.coin_1.x"). Every value is a 2-element [min,max] number tuple. VERIFY-2 reads this map to enumerate the perturbation envelope.
title: VERIFY-1 blueprint artifact (hardened, approved design)
description: The frozen, approved design emitted by VERIFY-1 (Design Critic). Extends gdd.schema.json: carries all gdd fields (config now REQUIRED and complete) plus layout/coupling/feasibility/referenceSolution/acceptanceCriteria/declaredRanges/verdict. Downstream nodes W2/W4/VERIFY-2 read this as single source of truth.

meta.description: Game identity. archetype/coreLoop/coreVerb are carried verbatim from classification.json; title is derived by W1.

archetype.description: Copied from classification.json. Routes W2 to templates/modules/<archetype>.

subMode.description: Optional within-archetype variant hint for W4 (e.g. grid_logic: 'puzzle'|'tactics'; top_down: 'arena'). Advisory in v1.

entities.description: Every game object. Player MUST be entity[0] (role:'player').

config.description: REQUIRED and COMPLETE in the blueprint. Tuning numbers W2 merges into the template gameConfig.json. Keys must match the archetype's config schema. Per-archetype valid keys — platformer: gravityY, jumpPower, walkSpeed, maxHealth, attackDamage, hurtingDuration, invulnerableTime; top_down: walkSpeed, dashSpeed, dashDuration, dashCooldown, maxHealth, attackDamage; grid_logic: cellSize, gridWidth, gridHeight, maxMoves, animationSpeed, inputDebounceMs; tower_defense: startingGold, startingLives, cellSize, timeBetweenWaves, sellRefundRate; ui_heavy: playerMaxHP, enemyMaxHP, handSize, comboTiers.

milestones.description: Carried from gdd, hardened against concrete numbers. 2–5 playable vertical slices.

layout.description: Concrete level placement. The level is fully determined — no missing coordinate. Supports both continuous (x/y) and discrete (gridX/gridY) archetypes via open coordinate objects.

goal.description: The win target entity: id + position. x/y for continuous archetypes; gridX/gridY for grid_logic.

rewards.description: Collectibles and intermediate targets. Each has an id + position.

threats.description: Hazards, enemies, patrollers. Each has id + kind + position or route + timing.

bounds.description: Level dimensions in pixels (continuous) or cells (grid_logic).

coupling.description: Threat-on-reward-path proof (§4). Every reward/goal must have at least one entry proving a threat contests it. VERIFY-2 reads this to confirm the build recreates the tension.

for.description: The reward or goal id this coupling entry describes (e.g. 'coin_1', 'exit').

threat.description: The threat entity id that contests it (e.g. 'patrol_1').

meetsAt.description: Coordinates where the player path intersects the threat region.

passableWindowMs.description: The timing window in ms during which the reward/goal is safely reachable (from §3 math). Must be > 0 for the design to be winnable+fair.

note.description: Human-readable rationale: why the threat is on this path and what the decision is.

feasibility.description: The kinematic/reachability math (§3). All numbers recorded for human audit. Archetype selects which math applies.

checks.description: One entry per required traversal/reach/survival window. Each records what was checked, the computed bounds, the required value, and the verdict.

what.description: What was checked (e.g. 'gap@x=320 width 96px', 'reach time to coin_1', 'BFS path length to goal', 'wave1 survivability DPS check').

computed.description: The derived values from the math (e.g. {h_max:159.8, d_max:206.7} or {shortestPath:14, D_total:120}).

required.description: The design's constraint that was checked against (e.g. {gap:96} or {maxMoves:20} or {wave1HP:100}).

verdict.description: PASS = satisfies as-is; FAIL = not satisfiable (design_failed); HARDENED = VERIFY-1 adjusted the number to make it pass.

numbersUsed.description: Every concrete input number used in the computation (e.g. {v:200, vy0:620, g:1200}). Enables a human to re-run the arithmetic.

referenceSolution.description: Proof-by-existence that the design is winnable AND that winning requires engaging the threat (§3). VERIFY-2 replays this against the build.

winsVia.description: How the win is achieved (e.g. 'controls', 'BFS_solution', 'optimal_tower_placement'). Declares the replay strategy for VERIFY-2.

steps.description: Ordered action sequence. Each step has an 'input' (a documented control or event) and exactly one or more outcome keys.

items.description: One step in the reference solution. 'input' is always required; at least one of reaches/clears/engagesThreat/observe+expect must be present to make the step checkable.

input.description: The player action (e.g. 'keyHold ArrowRight 600ms', 'keyPress ArrowUp', 'navigate to coin_1 when patrol_1 at x<=400', 'BFS move sequence: U,R,R,D', 'place tower at cell (3,2)').

reaches.description: A landmark or coordinate the player reaches after this step (e.g. 'gap@x=320', 'coin_1@(420,300)').

clears.description: An obstacle or gap that is cleared/passed (e.g. 'gap (peak 140 > spike row 120)', 'wave 1').

engagesThreat.description: The threat id that is engaged at this step (proves the decision is not avoided).

observe.description: A __GAME__ observable to check after this step (e.g. 'status', 'score', 'moveCount').

expect.description: The expected value of the observable (e.g. 'won', 3, true). Used when 'observe' is present.

engagesEveryThreat.description: True iff the reference solution engages every declared threat at least once (proving no threat is trivially avoidable in the intended play).

acceptanceCriteria.description: Given/When/Then acceptance criteria expressed in the window.__GAME__ observable vocabulary (§3 of template-contract.md). VERIFY-2's fidelity contract — checked from known preconditions.

milestone.description: The milestone this AC belongs to (e.g. 'M3').

given.description: Precondition in concrete terms (e.g. 'player at spawn (32,300), score 3 (all coins collected)'). Must reference hardened coordinates so VERIFY-2 can reproduce the state.

when.description: The trigger/action (e.g. 'fire controls toward exit per referenceSolution', 'press ArrowUp', 'wave 1 ends').

then.description: The expected observable outcome as a __GAME__ expression (e.g. "__GAME__.status === 'won'", "__GAME__.score >= 3").

expect.description: Exactly one comparator key. Must use the same comparator vocabulary as gdd assertions.

declaredRanges.description: THE PERTURBATION ENVELOPE — a flat map from parameterPath (dotted string) to [min,max] 2-tuple. VERIFY-2 enumerates this map, permutes each parameter within its declared band, and re-runs acceptanceCriteria; a faithful build is invariant, a hard-coded build diverges. Every declared range MUST keep §3 feasibility + §4 threat-on-path properties true at BOTH endpoints. Example paths: 'config.walkSpeed', 'config.gravityY', 'layout.rewards.coin_1.x', 'layout.threats.patrol_1.speed', 'layout.playerSpawn.x'.

verdict.description: The hard gate verdict. result is the machine-parseable field the workflow driver asserts. DESIGN_FAILED routes back to W1.

result.description: The hard gate (criteria 1-4 on the numbers). DESIGN_FAILED routes back to W1, never to W4.

rubric.description: Per-criterion record. One entry per rubric criterion (1-7).

verdict.description: PASS = satisfies as-is; FAIL = not satisfiable (triggers DESIGN_FAILED); HARDENED = VERIFY-1 fixed the design to pass.

evidence.description: The named principle + the observation (restating the design's purpose and judging against it, not a preference).

numbersUsed.description: Concrete numbers referenced in the evidence (for criteria 1-4 math-based checks).

hardened.description: What VERIFY-1 made concrete that W1 left implicit (e.g. 'filled patrol_1 route+speed', 'added respawn flow').

reasons.description: On DESIGN_FAILED: the specific criterion + numbers that could not be satisfied. Empty array on DESIGN_PASSED.

coordinate.description: A position. For continuous archetypes: x/y in pixels. For grid_logic: gridX/gridY in cell indices. Both may be present. Additional keys allowed for archetype-specific data.

placedEntity.description: A named entity at a concrete position (reward, obstacle, etc.). id is required; position may be x/y (continuous) or gridX/gridY (grid_logic) or both.

threat.description: A threat entity. 'kind' selects the motion model. Patrol threats carry route+speed+periodMs. Static hazards carry x/y only. Chasers carry speed. Grid threats carry gridX/gridY. Additional keys allowed per archetype.

kind.description: Motion/behavior model. patrol=waypoint loop; static_hazard=no motion; chaser=follows player; turret=ranged; wave_enemy=spawned on wave; grid_blocker=occupies cells.

speed.description: Movement speed in px/s (continuous archetypes) or cells/turn (grid).

periodMs.description: Full patrol cycle duration in ms (computed from route length + speed, or explicitly set). Must keep the §3 passable window > 0.

range.description: A [min, max] perturbation band for a parameter. Both endpoints must keep §3 feasibility and §4 threat-on-path properties true.

assertion.description: Executable runtime check (carried from gdd milestones, hardened with blueprint coordinates). W5/VERIFY-2 fires input and asserts observe against expect.
